metrics.name: Accounting correctness
metrics.desc:
Version in scope,Morpho V2 / MarketV2 / Morpho Blue core variable-rate market.
Accounting model type,Isolated variable-rate lending market.
Deposits represented as,User supply shares.
Borrows represented as,User borrow shares.
Collateral represented as,User collateral balance.
Interest accrual mechanism,Market-level accrueInterest.
Market state fields,total supply assets/shares, total borrow assets/shares, last update, fee.
Reserve accounting model,Fee-recipient supply position; no scored reserve solvency claim.
Fee accrual mechanism,Governance-set market fee; fee recipient receives fees via supply position.
Current fee status,Official docs state no fees currently applied.
Bad debt accounting,Market-level outcome; excluded from protocol-level scoring.
Shared accounting logic across markets,Single core logic for markets.
Market-specific accounting customizations,Five immutable market parameters only.
Use math-sensitive logic,Asset/share conversions, interest indices, price scaling, rounding.
Upgradeable,Core contract immutable.
Known historical accounting bugs,No unresolved Morpho V1 core accounting issue found in reviewed sources.
Out-of-scope historical issue,Legacy optimizer index-caching vulnerability, mitigated, V0 context.
Operation evidence at scale,Large Morpho ecosystem TVL and active loans; maturity proxy only.
Public documentation,Official docs, repository, audits, formal verification.

metrics.name: Accounting correctness
metrics.desc:
Share-based accounting is simple, unified, and documented at core-market level.
Core logic is immutable, reducing upgrade-induced accounting drift.
Formal verification, audits, fuzzing, and invariant testing support a below-average protocol accounting risk.
Score is not 1 because the accounting is math-sensitive and depends on token, oracle, and IRM assumptions documented by the protocol.
Market bad debt is excluded from this anchor unless caused by protocol-level accounting failure.

metrics.name: Liquidation reliability
metrics.desc:
Liquidation trigger mechanism,LTV above LLTV.
Health metric,Health factor below 1.
Liquidation executor,Permissionless external liquidators.
Liquidation type,Standard full or partial liquidation in core.
Close factor,Up to 100% of debt.
Liquidation incentive,LLTV-based liquidation incentive factor.
Liquidation fee,No protocol liquidation fee in standard liquidation docs.
Liquidation ordering,First-come-first-served; no auction.
Liquidator participation,Bots and external liquidators required.
Oracle dependency,Market oracle price.
Market parameter dependency,LLTV and oracle are immutable market parameters.
Core implementation,liquidate function in Morpho core.
External liquidation dependencies,RPC, wallet, bots, profitability.
Known historical liquidation bugs,No unresolved Morpho V1 core liquidation bug found in reviewed sources.
Market-level liquidation failures,Excluded from direct protocol scoring.

metrics.name: Liquidation reliability
metrics.desc:
Core liquidation logic is explicit, permissionless, and integrated into the protocol.
Liquidation reliability depends on external liquidator action; the protocol does not perform liquidations itself.
The mechanism depends directly on oracle correctness and immutable LLTV selection.
First-come-first-served liquidation is simpler than auctions but introduces MEV and execution-race dependence.
Market-specific bad debt or insufficient liquidation depth is excluded from this protocol score.

metrics.name: Oracle integrity
metrics.desc:
Oracle model,Oracle-agnostic.
Oracle address mutability,Immutable per market.
Oracle interface,price() function.
Price output format,Collateral price quoted in loan token with protocol scaling.
Reference oracle implementation,MorphoChainlinkOracleV2.
Supported oracle source types,Price-feed, exchange-rate, fixed-price patterns.
Oracle selection responsibility,Market creators and curators, not Morpho core.
Oracle governance control,No core oracle whitelist identified in reviewed docs.
Oracle failure behavior,Oracle reverts or incorrect values can affect liveness or correctness.
Price scaling assumptions,Oracle must return correct scaling.
Known protocol-level oracle bugs,No unresolved Morpho V1 core oracle-interface bug found in reviewed sources.
Known market-level oracle incidents,PAXG/USDC oracle misconfiguration; cUSDO/USDC LP oracle manipulation.
Public documentation,Official oracle documentation and reference implementation.

metrics.name: Oracle integrity
metrics.desc:
The core oracle interface is intentionally minimal and immutable per market.
Oracle-agnostic design avoids a single enshrined oracle dependency, but delegates correctness to market configuration.
Price scaling and oracle liveness are explicit protocol-level assumptions.
Market-level oracle incidents are not scored as direct protocol failures, but they support the importance of oracle-interface and configuration risk.
Score is not higher because Morpho core does not itself issue prices or guarantee asset redemption.

metrics.name: Governance and upgrade safety
metrics.desc:
Core contract upgradeability,Immutable.
Market parameter mutability,Immutable after market creation.
Owner powers,Owner transfer, IRM enablement, LLTV enablement, fee setting, fee recipient.
Fee cap,25% of borrower interest.
IRM control,Governance can whitelist IRMs.
LLTV control,Governance can whitelist LLTVs.
Oracle control,Oracle chosen per market; no reviewed core oracle whitelist.
Governance voting mechanism,MORPHO token voting through Snapshot.
Proposal threshold,500,000 MORPHO delegated or held.
Execution mechanism,Governance actions implemented by 5-of-9 multisig.
Token upgradeability,Wrapped MORPHO token upgradeable.
Pause powers,No core market pause power identified in reviewed official governance powers.
Asset seizure powers,No core user-asset seizure power identified in reviewed official governance powers.
Documented governance scope,Treasury, token, fee switch, LLTV/IRM whitelisting, ENS/license.
Known governance execution issues,No unresolved Morpho V1 core governance-execution issue found in reviewed sources.

metrics.name: Governance and upgrade safety
metrics.desc:
Immutable core markets materially reduce upgrade-risk default vectors.
Governance powers are documented and narrower than full protocol upgrade control.
Fee, IRM whitelist, and LLTV whitelist powers can affect future or active market economics, so governance risk is not negligible.
Token upgradeability and multisig execution are scored mainly in control modifiers, not double-counted here.
No evidence found of unsafe core upgrades, governance capture event, or opaque protocol-level execution.

metrics.name: Upgradeability and admin control
metrics.desc:
Core upgradeable,No.
Market upgradeable,No; markets immutable.
Market params mutable,No after creation.
Owner powers,Yes; fee, fee recipient, LLTV/IRM enablement, owner transfer.
Admin fee control,Yes; capped.
Admin risk-parameter control,Limited to whitelisting LLTVs and IRMs.
Admin oracle control,No reviewed core oracle whitelist.
Pause control,Not identified for core markets.
Upgrade delay,Not applicable to immutable core.
Cross-chain admin surface,Multiple deployments; chain-hard-fork assumptions outside Morpho control.

metrics.name: Upgradeability and admin control
metrics.desc:
Core immutability lowers protocol-level default risk.
Immutable market parameters reduce post-creation admin intervention risk.
Residual owner powers justify a moderate, not extreme, reduction.
Multi-chain deployment adds some residual operational surface.

metrics.name: Governance execution integrity
metrics.desc:
Proposal venue,Morpho governance forum.
Voting venue,Snapshot.
Voting token,MORPHO.
Proposal threshold,500,000 MORPHO delegated or held.
Execution wallet,Governance multisig.
Execution threshold,5 of 9.
Timelock,Not identified in reviewed official governance docs.
Onchain transparency,Multisig addresses documented.
Procedure documentation,Governance scope and process documented.
Known execution failures,No unresolved core governance-execution issue found in reviewed sources.

metrics.name: Governance execution integrity
metrics.desc:
Governance execution process is documented.
Snapshot plus multisig execution is operationally clear.
Absence of a reviewed explicit timelock prevents a risk-reducing score.
No evidence found of core governance execution failure.

metrics.name: Governance power concentration
metrics.desc:
Voting model,Token-weighted MORPHO governance.
Token max supply,1,000,000,000 MORPHO.
Governance-controlled allocation,35.4% in official allocation schedule.
Strategic partner allocation,27.5% in official allocation schedule.
Founder allocation,15.2% in official allocation schedule.
Circulating supply schedule,Transferability enabled on 21 November 2024.
Delegation model,Delegation and voting through MORPHO governance flow.
Quorum,Not identified in reviewed official governance docs.
Concentration signal,Material structural concentration.
Counterbalancing controls,Immutable core and limited governance scope.

metrics.name: Governance power concentration
metrics.desc:
Token-weighted voting and allocation structure create governance concentration risk.
Immutable core reduces the impact of concentrated voting power.
Concentration affects future parameter whitelisting, fees, treasury, and token matters more than existing core-market code.
This risk is applied here rather than over-penalizing the governance anchor.

metrics.name: Operational complexity
metrics.desc:
Core architecture,Minimal immutable singleton core.
Contract footprint,Main core logic concentrated in Morpho.sol with internal libraries.
Market architecture,Isolated markets.
External dependencies,ERC20 tokens, oracle, IRM.
Parameter count,Five market parameters.
Cross-chain deployments,Multiple EVM deployments.
Callback support,Present in core actions.
Flash loan support,Present; zero flash fee.
Periphery dependency,Periphery not used by core.
Token compatibility assumptions,No fee-on-transfer; compliant token assumptions.
Oracle/IRM liveness assumptions,Oracle or IRM reverts can affect liveness.

metrics.name: Operational complexity
metrics.desc:
Core architecture is deliberately simple and immutable.
Isolated markets reduce cross-market contagion at protocol-accounting level.
External oracle, IRM, token, callback, and flash-loan surfaces prevent a stronger reduction.
Cross-chain deployment adds operational surface but not a core-design failure by itself.

metrics.name: Privileged roles scope
metrics.desc:
Owner role,Yes.
Governance role,Yes.
Multisig role,5-of-9 execution.
Fee role,Governance/owner can set market fee up to cap.
IRM whitelist role,Governance/owner can enable IRMs.
LLTV whitelist role,Governance/owner can enable LLTVs.
Oracle whitelist role,Not identified in core docs.
Market creator role,Permissionless within enabled LLTV/IRM constraints.
Pauser role,Not identified for core markets.
Asset recovery role,No core user-asset seizure role identified in reviewed docs.
Token admin role,Wrapped MORPHO upgradeable.
Rewards/admin roles,Out of scope.

metrics.name: Privileged roles scope
metrics.desc:
Privileged roles exist but are narrower than full upgrade or custody powers.
No reviewed evidence of core pause, seizure, or market-parameter rewrite powers.
Fee and whitelist powers retain some protocol-level governance impact.
Token-admin scope is relevant to governance but not core market accounting.

metrics.name: Audits
metrics.desc:
Core audit count,Multiple Morpho V1 reviews listed.
Core audit firms,OpenZeppelin, Spearbit, Cantina.
Contest coverage,Cantina Morpho V1 competition.
Pre-deployment review,External reviews before deployment.
Post-deployment review,Additional listed reviews and contests.
Periphery audit separation,Separate vault/periphery audits listed.
Audit reports public,Public audits page and repository references.
Critical unresolved findings,No unresolved Morpho V1 core critical issue found in reviewed sources.
Scope match to Morpho V1,Strong for core.

metrics.name: Audits
metrics.desc:
Multiple reputable security reviews cover Morpho V1 core.
Audit coverage is public and version-specific.
Contest coverage adds breadth beyond firm audits.
Audits reduce but do not eliminate protocol-level implementation risk.

metrics.name: Formal verification
metrics.desc:
Core FV coverage,Morpho V1 core logic.
FV providers,Certora and Halmos.
FV tooling,Certora, Halmos.
Verified invariants,Core accounting and safety invariants.
Public FV artifacts,Formal verification reports listed publicly.
Liquidation FV coverage,Pre-liquidation core logic formally verified; opt-in pre-liquidation is not core standard liquidation.
Oracle FV coverage,Interface assumptions remain market-dependent.
Coverage limitations,Market configuration and external oracle correctness not fully eliminated.
Scope match to Morpho V1,Strong for core logic.

metrics.name: Formal verification
metrics.desc:
Formal verification directly covers Morpho V1 core logic.
Independent tools and providers improve assurance.
Verification is especially relevant to accounting and state-transition correctness.
External oracle and token assumptions remain outside full FV protection.

metrics.name: Testing
metrics.desc:
Unit tests,Yes.
Integration tests,Yes.
Fork tests,Yes.
Fuzz tests,Yes.
Mutation tests,Yes.
Branch coverage target,All branches targeted.
Public test suite,Repository public.
CI status,Not scored.
Core logic coverage,Strong documented coverage.
Scope match to Morpho V1,Strong.

metrics.name: Testing
metrics.desc:
Testing stack is broad across unit, integration, fork, fuzz, and mutation testing.
Branch-targeted testing is especially useful for compact core logic.
Public repository improves reproducibility.
Testing cannot fully cover adversarial market configuration or external oracle failures.

metrics.name: Monitoring
metrics.desc:
Invariant monitoring,Continuous invariant bots described.
Market-level monitoring,Invariants checked after market transactions.
Transaction-level monitoring,Yes, per security framework.
Circuit breakers,No circuit breakers by design.
Incident response process,Documented security-process framework.
Approval monitoring,Added after 2025 app incident.
Governance monitoring,Not separately scored.
Public dashboards,DefiLlama ecosystem data available.
Liquidator monitoring,Permissionless liquidator ecosystem.
Scope match to Morpho V1,Moderate to strong.

metrics.name: Monitoring
metrics.desc:
Invariant monitoring improves detection of protocol-level anomalies.
No circuit breakers limits ability to stop failures after detection.
Permissionless liquidation means monitoring does not guarantee liquidation execution.
App-level remediation is useful operational context but not core protocol control.

metrics.name: Incident history
metrics.desc:
Core exploit history,No unresolved Morpho V1 core exploit found in reviewed sources.
Accounting incident history,No unresolved Morpho V1 core accounting incident found in reviewed sources.
Liquidation incident history,No unresolved Morpho V1 core liquidation incident found in reviewed sources.
Oracle incident history,Market-level oracle incidents identified; direct scoring excluded.
Governance incident history,No unresolved Morpho V1 core governance incident found in reviewed sources.
Frontend/app incidents,April 2025 app configuration incident; smart contracts unaffected; no funds lost.
Market-level incidents,cUSDO/USDC and PAXG/USDC market-level oracle incidents.
Legacy-version incidents,June 2023 optimizer/index issue; out of scope.
Funds lost at protocol level,No Morpho V1 core protocol-level loss identified in reviewed sources.
Unresolved protocol-level issues,None identified in reviewed sources.

metrics.name: Incident history
metrics.desc:
No reviewed unresolved core exploit materially supports the protocol.
Market-level oracle losses show real operational risk around market configuration, but are excluded from direct protocol PD.
Frontend/app incident did not affect smart contracts, but modestly weakens operational assurance.
Legacy optimizer issue is out of scope but relevant as historical learning context.

metrics.name: Operational changes history
metrics.desc:
Core upgrade history,Core MarketV1 immutable; no core upgrade path.
Token migration history,Legacy MORPHO to wrapped MORPHO.
Governance changes,Transferability enabled on 21 November 2024.
Security-process changes,Security framework documented.
Incident-remediation changes,SDK fixes, review process, approval monitoring after app incident.
Deprecated architecture,Morpho V0 / optimizers out of scope.
Cross-chain deployment changes,Multiple chain deployments documented.
Peripheral contract changes,Vaults, adapters, bundlers out of scope.
Change-management transparency,Governance docs and forum process documented.
Scope match to Morpho V1,Moderate.

metrics.name: Operational changes history
metrics.desc:
Core immutability means operational changes mainly affect governance, periphery, token, and app surfaces.
Documented security and remediation processes support neutral-to-positive assurance.
Token and app changes are relevant but not direct core-market changes.
No adjustment beyond neutral is applied to avoid double counting immutability and audits.

metrics.name: System maturity and production track record
metrics.desc:
Production deployment,Core contracts deployed across multiple EVM networks.
Multi-chain footprint,Ethereum and multiple L2 / EVM deployments documented.
Public code,GitHub repository public.
Public docs,Official docs public.
Production TVL proxy,DefiLlama reports large combined Morpho TVL; maturity proxy only.
Active loans proxy,DefiLlama reports large combined active loans; maturity proxy only.
Time in production,Public Morpho Blue code and security materials date from 2023–2024.
Security program maturity,Audits, FV, testing, monitoring, contests.
Governance maturity,Documented governance process and multisig.
Ecosystem dependency,High; not scored as market solvency.
Scope limitations,TVL includes broader Morpho ecosystem, not only one market.

metrics.name: System maturity and production track record
metrics.desc:
Public code, public docs, and public security artifacts support maturity.
Large production usage is relevant as operational evidence, not market solvency evidence.
Multi-chain deployment shows operational depth but also increases environment surface.
Track record supports a moderate assurance reduction.

metrics.name: Anchor scoring
metrics.desc:
Anchor,Score,Anchor PD
Accounting correctness,2,0.50%
Liquidation reliability,3,2.00%
Oracle integrity,3,2.00%
Governance and upgrade safety,2,0.50%

metrics.name: Control modifiers scoring
metrics.desc:
Modifier,Score
Upgradeability and admin control,0.90
Governance execution integrity,1.00
Governance power concentration,1.08
Operational complexity,0.92
Privileged roles scope,0.95

metrics.desc:
Multiplier,Score
Audits,0.92
Formal verification,0.90
Testing,0.92
Monitoring,0.97
Incidents history,1.02
Operational changes history,1.00
